question: PBQ: Change management workflow order
note: Change management protects production systems by requiring planning, approval, testing, rollback, and documentation.
instructions: A technician needs to update software on production computers. Put the change-management steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Create a change request
Document risk, impact, and affected systems
Get approval from the appropriate authority
Schedule a maintenance window
Test the change and prepare a rollback plan
Implement the approved change
Verify functionality and document the outcome

question: PBQ: Documentation artifact selection
note: Different documentation types solve different operational needs.
instructions: Match each operational need to the best documentation artifact.

items.scenario: Track a user's reported issue from start to finish
Record what changed during a maintenance window
Track assigned hardware and serial numbers
Show how switches, routers, and servers connect
Provide repeatable steps for common fixes
List who to contact during an outage

question: PBQ: Safety control selection
note: Operational safety includes electrical safety, ESD prevention, PPE, fire safety, and environmental handling.
instructions: Match each safety scenario to the best control or practice.

items.scenario: Prevent static discharge while replacing RAM
Protect eyes while cutting cable or working with debris
Handle a swollen lithium-ion battery
Work inside a power supply
Suppress an electrical fire
Avoid tripping over temporary cabling

question: PBQ: Professional communication workflow
note: Professionalism means listening, setting expectations, avoiding blame, and following through.
instructions: A frustrated user reports that their computer is unusable before a meeting. Put the communication steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Listen actively and let the user explain the issue
Show empathy and acknowledge the impact
Ask clarifying questions to determine urgency and scope
Set realistic expectations for next steps and timing
Provide a workaround if available
Follow up and document the interaction

question: PBQ: Backup type selection
note: Full backups copy everything; incremental copies changes since the last backup; differential copies changes since the last full backup.
instructions: Match each backup scenario to the best backup or recovery concept.

items.scenario: Copy all selected data every time
Copy changes since the last backup of any type
Copy changes since the last full backup
Capture a point-in-time state of a system or volume
Confirm backups can actually be used
Plan for recovery after a major outage

question: PBQ: Backup restoration order
note: Restoration should be planned, verified, and documented to avoid overwriting good data or restoring the wrong version.
instructions: A user needs a folder restored from backup. Put the restore process in the best order.
correctOrder: Verify the user's identity and restore request
Identify the correct file or folder and restore date
Confirm whether current data must be preserved first
Restore the requested data to the proper location or alternate location
Have the user verify the restored data
Document the restore action

question: PBQ: Licensing model selection
note: Licensing questions test whether the license is tied to hardware, a user, an organization, source code rights, or recurring payment.
instructions: Match each software licensing scenario to the best license type.

items.scenario: License preinstalled and tied to a specific computer
License purchased by an individual from a store
Organization purchases licenses in bulk
Software source code can be viewed and modified under its license
Software access continues only while recurring payments are active

question: PBQ: Environmental disposal selection
note: Electronic waste, batteries, toner, and hazardous materials should follow approved disposal or recycling procedures.
instructions: Match each item to the best disposal or handling method.

items.scenario: Used toner cartridge
Old laptop battery
Retired desktop computer
Hard drive containing company data
Chemical cleaning product
Mobile phone with swollen battery

question: PBQ: Incident escalation decision
note: Escalate when the issue exceeds your permissions, skill level, time limit, scope, or safety boundaries.
instructions: Match each situation to the best next action.

items.scenario: Issue exceeds technician permissions
User needs immediate work continuity
Technician changed a setting successfully
Problem may affect multiple users
Safety hazard is discovered
items.answer: Stop Work and Follow Safety Procedure

question: PBQ: Ticket handling workflow
note: Good tickets preserve history, accountability, user communication, and final resolution details.
instructions: A support ticket comes in for a user who cannot access a shared drive. Put the ticket workflow in the best order.
correctOrder: Review the ticket details and contact the user if needed
Confirm the user's identity and scope of the problem
Troubleshoot permissions, network connectivity, and mapped drive status
Apply the fix or escalate with notes
Verify the user can access the share
Update and close the ticket with resolution notes
